name: sf-doctor
description: Binary-assertion suite for /sf:doctor. Covers the five always-on status sections, install-mode skipping, and the standalone read-only permission audit (--permissions, 'KEYS ON YOUR RING'). The permission-audit assertions are concretely backed by the hermetic scripts/tests/test_check_permissions.sh (which seeds fake tokens and asserts they never appear in the output).

tests.expected_output_summary: Five sections render; a final summary line; read-only throughout.
binary_assertions: Output contains all five section headers: ENVIRONMENT, PLUGINS, SCHEMA VERSIONS, FRAMEWORK UPDATE, BACKUP
A crashing or timed-out check-script degrades to a per-section failure note and does NOT crash the overall report
The run is side-effect-free: nothing under the wiki or settings is written
Exit code is 0 when no blocker (❌) is present

tests.expected_output_summary: FRAMEWORK UPDATE section is skipped (no marketplace fetch).
binary_assertions: FRAMEWORK UPDATE section reports skipped (install mode) and performs no gh api network call

tests.fixture: scripts/tests/test_check_permissions.sh (hermetic; seeds a crafted .claude.json + settings.json with fake tokens)
tests.expected_output_summary: Standalone 'KEYS ON YOUR RING' report: MCP servers by name+transport, allow/deny/ask tallies, broad grants flagged, plugins + hooks listed, and ZERO secret values printed.
binary_assertions: Output contains the report header 'KEYS ON YOUR RING' and the framing copy 'keys != instructions' (keys ≠ instructions)
Every configured MCP server (global ~/.claude.json#mcpServers and per-project projects.*.mcpServers) is listed by name with its transport classified as stdio, http, or sse
permissions.allow / permissions.deny / permissions.ask are each tallied with a rule count and per-prefix breakdown, including a settings.local.json overlay when present
Broad grants are flagged: bare `Bash` (every shell command) and `mcp__*` (every tool on every server); server-scoped `mcp__<server>__*` wildcards are noted
enabledPlugins (a dict keyed '<plugin>@<marketplace>') and configured hooks (by event name) are listed
No secret/env/token/header value is ever printed: a seeded fake token present in an MCP server's env, in an http server's headers, and in a project server's env is ABSENT from the output
All inputs absent (no ~/.claude.json, no settings.json, no settings.local.json) still renders a clean empty report and exits 0
The audit performs no writes and no network calls
